project_name: Vision-Based Smart Classroom Environment Monitoring System

design_philosophy.description: A high-precision, data-dense interface inspired by airport control towers and medical imaging software. It prioritizes clarity, rapid scanning, and objective truth. It avoids decorative fluff in favor of functional density and high-contrast status indicators.

usage_rules.headings: Use Manrope for all section titles and card headers. Weight: 600 or 700. Tracking: tight.
usage_rules.body: Use Inter for all standard text. Weight: 400 or 500. Leading: relaxed.
usage_rules.data: Use JetBrains Mono for ALL numerical data (occupancy counts, percentages, timestamps, confidence scores). This distinguishes 'data' from 'content'.

colors.rules: Use white backgrounds for the main dashboard in light mode to maintain a 'clinical' feel. In dark mode, use Zinc-950, never pure black. Status colors must be used sparingly as indicators (dots, borders, badges), not as full backgrounds.

visual_enhancers.viewfinder_corners: Add SVG 'L' shaped brackets to the corners of classroom image containers to simulate a camera viewfinder.
visual_enhancers.scan_lines: Use a subtle vertical scanning line animation (CSS gradient moving top to bottom) on 'Live' video feeds.
visual_enhancers.glassmorphism: Use 'Crystal Glass' style for the sidebar navigation and floating action bars. Light mode: bg-white/80 backdrop-blur-md. Dark mode: bg-zinc-900/80 backdrop-blur-md.
visual_enhancers.borders: Use 'Grid Borders' style. Tables and grids should have subtle 1px borders (zinc-200/zinc-800) to separate data points without clutter.
visual_enhancers.shadows: Minimal shadows. Use 'shadow-sm' generally. Use 'shadow-md' only for floating elements.

layout_strategy.dashboard_grid: Mode B: High Density Grid. Use a strict bento grid (grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-3 lg:grid-cols-4). Gap: gap-4 or gap-6. Every pixel must be utilized.
layout_strategy.containers: Cardless or 'Technical Card' style. Rectangular with very slight rounding (rounded-md or rounded-sm). No large rounded corners (rounded-xl is forbidden).
layout_strategy.spacing: Dense. Use p-4 for standard cards. Use p-6 for major sections. Avoid excessive whitespace in data views; keep related metrics close.

cards.modification: Remove excessive shadow. Add a 'data-status' border logic (e.g., border-l-4 border-l-red-500 for alerts).

charts.style: Minimalist. Hide grid lines where possible. Use thin strokes. Tooltips must be high-contrast.

vision_overlay.description: A custom component to overlay bounding boxes and confidence scores on images.
vision_overlay.style: Border: 2px solid [StatusColor]. Label: Background [StatusColor] Text White text-xs.

motion.hover: Subtle scale (scale-[1.01]) or border highlight. No massive jumps.
motion.loading: Skeleton loaders (Shadcn) + localized spinners for data fetching.

classroom_occupied.description: Students occupied in a lecture hall.

classroom_messy.description: Messy floor with crumpled paper (Cleanliness Alert).

instructions_to_main_agent: Implement a 'Dashboard Layout' with a persistent sidebar.
The Home page should be the 'Overview Dashboard' summarizing all classrooms.
Use 'Recharts' for: Occupancy Trends (Line Chart), Cleanliness Scores (Bar Chart), Lighting Levels (Area Chart).
Create a 'CameraFeed' component that takes an image URL and draws SVG overlays (bounding boxes) to simulate CV detection.
Use 'Sonner' for real-time alerts (e.g., 'Room 302 needs cleaning').
Ensure the 'Upload' page allows drag-and-drop analysis simulation.
Use the provided image URLs to mock the camera feeds.
The 'Settings' page should allow threshold configuration for alerts.
universal_guidelines: Do not use gradients for backgrounds. Keep surfaces flat and clean.
Ensure text contrast meets WCAG AA standards (4.5:1).
Do not use generic 'Inter' for headings; use 'Manrope'.
Make sure the 'Analysis Results' are visually distinct from the rest of the UI (e.g., use a dark panel for the image analysis result even in light mode).
